utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all five of these items. Each one is fully specified with detailed coverage descriptions and associated costs (monthly_cost or total cost breakdown). Therefore, all items are included and properly detailed.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes four main components: health_coverage (Essential Compliance Plan6), property_coverage, travel_coverage, and additional_services. To map ratings, I must identify the equivalent ground-truth entries. 'Essential Compliance Plan6' covers both physical and mental health with preventive care, prescription, psychiatry, telemedicine, and gym reimbursement at €100/month and 5% deductible. Among ground-truth options, the closest is 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' (physical rating 9, cost 100, 5% deductible, gym reimbursement) and for mental health, 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' (mental rating 4). For property_coverage (€40/month) with electronics protection, natural disaster, and temporary accommodation, the best match is 'InsurePlus Standard Coverage' (rating 10, similar benefits). For travel_coverage (€30/month) with adventure sports and evacuation, the nearest equivalent is 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' (travel rating 10, includes adventure activity coverage). For additional_services (disability insurance, wellness programs, telemedicine, €10/month), the telemedicine portion maps to 'Telemedicine Services' (rating 10), and 'Wellness Programs' (rating 3) also appears in the ground-truth list. Disability insurance does not have a separate rating, so we omit it. Therefore, the mapped ratings are: LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical 9, mental 4), InsurePlus Standard Coverage (10), InsurePlus Standard Plan (10), Telemedicine Services (10), and Wellness Programs (3).
